summary: Autonomous healing of training data caused quality regression from 6.7/10 to 4.5/10

what_happened.trigger: 10 errors detected in Opus-mined training data via verification pipeline
what_happened.action_taken: Sent full files to Opus with instruction to rewrite and fix all 10 errors
what_happened.actual_outcome: Training data scored 4.5/10 — worse than original

root_cause_analysis.detail: Opus was asked to rewrite 29,151 chars of content but max_tokens=8192 produced only 14,393 chars — a 50.6% content loss
root_cause_analysis.detail_2: Instead of patching 10 specific lines, the entire file was regenerated, losing good content that had no bugs
contributing_factors: No content preservation check before overwriting original
No size comparison (new < old = red flag)
No section-by-section diff to detect lost content
No rollback mechanism when healed output is worse
Verification was run AFTER overwrite, not BEFORE commit

lessons_learned: NEVER rewrite entire files through LLM — apply surgical patches only
ALWAYS compare output size to input size — if smaller, flag it
ALWAYS verify BEFORE committing healed output, not after
ALWAYS keep a backup/snapshot before healing
Healing should use diff-based approach, not wholesale replacement
Token limits make LLMs unreliable for large-file rewriting
The immune system needs a pre-commit verification gate
Surgical fixes worked (6.7→6.8), wholesale rewrites failed (6.7→4.5)
system_gaps_exposed: No post-healing quality gate (verify before commit)
No automatic rollback on quality degradation
Healing coordinator doesn't diff old vs new
No 'healing made it worse' detection circuit

resolution.immediate: Restored original files from git, applied surgical patches manually
resolution.permanent: Build healing safeguards into the system (snapshot, verify, rollback)

kpi_impact.healing_success_rate: this counts as a failed heal + successful retry
kpi_impact.learning_value: HIGH — this failure teaches Grace how NOT to heal
